subject: Q2 Distillate Pricing — Need Commitment by Friday
body:
Hey Dustin,

Heads up — our THC distillate supplier is raising prices 12% starting April 1. I can lock in current pricing for your Q2 order but I need a commitment by this Friday (March 7).

Here's what I'm looking at for your usual volume:

- THC Distillate (90%+): $4,200/kg → holds at $3,750/kg if committed by Friday
- CBD Isolate: $1,100/kg → holds at $950/kg
- Broad Spectrum: $2,800/kg → holds at $2,500/kg

Your Q1 volume was 45kg distillate + 20kg CBD. Want to match or increase for Q2?

Let me know ASAP. Once Friday passes I can't hold these numbers.

Steve

subject: PO #4471 — 50,000 units Tropical Blast gummies
body:
Dustin,

Purchase Order #4471 attached. Summary:

- Product: Tropical Blast THC Gummies 10mg (your SKU TB-10-100ct)
- Quantity: 50,000 units (500 cases)
- Delivery: March 28 to our Denver distribution center
- Price: $2.40/unit per our MSA
- Payment: Net 30

This is our biggest single order yet — we're rolling into 340 new dispensary locations in Colorado and Oklahoma next month. Need to confirm you can hit the March 28 date. If there's any risk to that timeline, let me know now so I can adjust our retail launch.

Also — can you send updated COAs for the current batch? Two of our retail partners are asking.

Bob

subject: Gelatin shipment delayed — ETA pushed to March 10
body:
Dustin,

Bad news. The 200kg gelatin order (your PO IM-2026-0089) got held up at the port. Original ETA was March 5, now looking at March 10.

I know you've got production scheduled for the 8th. Options:

1. I can source 50kg from our secondary supplier (different grade, 240 bloom vs your usual 250) and overnight it. Gets there March 6. Cost bump is ~$800.
2. Wait for the full shipment March 10. Push your production 2 days.
3. I check if anyone in my network has 200kg of 250 bloom available for immediate ship. No guarantees.

What do you want me to do?

Steve

subject: Re: New SKU discussion — Sugar-free line
body:
Hey Dustin,

Following up on our call last week about the sugar-free line. My team ran the numbers and we think there's real demand here. The sugar-free gummy market is growing 23% YoY and none of the major cannabis brands have a credible product yet.

Here's what we'd need:
- 3 flavors to start (suggest: Watermelon, Mango, Mixed Berry)
- 5mg and 10mg options
- Target retail price: $24.99 for 30ct
- First production run: 10,000 units per SKU (60,000 total)
- Timeline: ready for our Q3 retail push (July 1)

Can Gummy Co handle the formulation? Do you need to bring in a food scientist or do you have that in-house?

I'd like to get an LOI signed by end of March so we can lock in shelf space with our retail partners.

Bob

subject: COA for Batch #IM-2026-THC-0223
body:
Dustin,

Attached is the Certificate of Analysis for your latest distillate batch:

- Batch: IM-2026-THC-0223
- THC: 91.3%
- CBD: 0.4%
- Total Cannabinoids: 94.2%
- Residual Solvents: ND
- Heavy Metals: All below LOQ
- Pesticides: ND (full panel, 96 analytes)
- Microbial: Pass

This batch tested clean across the board. Let me know if you need anything else for your Metrc entries.

Steve

subject: Customer complaint — potency inconsistency in Blueberry SKU
body:
Dustin,

Got a complaint from one of our Denver dispensaries (Green Leaf on Colfax). They're saying the Blueberry Bliss 10mg gummies from the last shipment (lot BLU-0215) are testing below label claim.

Their in-house testing showed 7.2mg per gummy vs the 10mg label. That's 28% below claim. Colorado allows +/- 15% variance.

I need:
1. Your internal QC results for lot BLU-0215
2. Third-party COA if you have one
3. A plan for the remaining inventory from that lot

This is time-sensitive. If the state gets involved it's a problem for both of us. Can you look into this today?

Bob
signals: action_item:pull QC results for lot BLU-0215

subject: New organic sweetener samples — try these?
body:
Hey Dustin,

We just got in a new organic erythritol + monk fruit blend from a supplier out of Thailand. Way better mouthfeel than the stevia blend you've been using for your low-sugar line.

I'm sending you 5kg of samples next week. No charge — just want your team's feedback. If it works for your formulation, pricing would be about $18/kg vs the $22/kg you're paying for the stevia blend.

No rush on this one. Just thought of you when I tasted the samples.

Steve

subject: Payment for January invoice #GC-2026-0112
body:
Dustin,

Just a heads up — our AP team processed payment for invoice #GC-2026-0112 ($87,400) yesterday. Should hit your account in 2-3 business days.

Also, February invoice (#GC-2026-0215, $62,100) is approved and in the queue. Net 30 puts that at March 17.

We're current on everything. Let me know if your records don't match.

Bob

subject: Re: Q2 Distillate Pricing — Need Commitment by Friday
body:
Dustin,

Just bumping this — Friday is tomorrow. If I don't hear back by 5pm ET I'll have to let the pricing expire.

I'd hate for you to get hit with the 12% increase. Your Q2 at the new rate would be ~$5,400 more than the locked price.

Call me if easier: (303) 555-0147

Steve

subject: Dispensary partner intro — Rocky Mountain Remedies
body:
Hey Dustin,

I want to introduce you to Jake Martinez, the buyer at Rocky Mountain Remedies. They're a 12-location chain in the Front Range and they've been asking if they can buy direct from you for their house brand.

I told them about your white-label capabilities and they're interested. This wouldn't conflict with our MSA since their house brand is a different SKU.

Looping Jake in on the next email. He's expecting to hear from you.

Bob
signals: introduction:Jake Martinez at Rocky Mountain Remedies

subject: Metrc transfer manifest #TR-2026-0891 — please confirm receipt
body:
Dustin,

Metrc is showing transfer #TR-2026-0891 as delivered to your facility but you haven't confirmed receipt yet. It's been 3 days — Metrc flags unconfirmed transfers after 72 hours.

Can you or your team confirm receipt in Metrc today? The manifest covers:
- 25kg THC Distillate (batch IM-2026-THC-0223)
- 10kg CBD Isolate (batch IM-2026-CBD-0218)

Tag numbers are on the manifest. Let me know if anything doesn't match.

Steve

subject: LinkedIn post about Gummy Co — want to tag you?
body:
Dustin,

I'm putting together a LinkedIn post about our supply chain partners for National Cannabis Industry Awareness week. Want to highlight the quality of your operation.

Draft:
"Proud to work with manufacturers who take quality seriously. @GummyCo's facility runs 99.7% potency accuracy across 2M+ units this year. That's the kind of partner you want when your brand is on the label."

Okay to post? Want me to tag your company page or your personal profile?

Bob

subject: Fwd: Industry newsletter — Hemp Grower Magazine March issue
body:
Thought you might find this interesting. The article on page 12 about extraction efficiency is worth a read.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
Hemp Grower Magazine — March 2026
In this issue: New extraction methods, state-by-state regulatory update, Q1 market pricing data...

subject: Out of office: March 3-5
body:
I'll be at the Cannabis Business Summit in Las Vegas March 3-5. Limited email access. For urgent PO issues contact my ops manager Sarah at sarah@cpgbrandco.com.

Bob

subject: Flavoring recall notice — batch FL-0198 (DOES NOT affect your orders)
body:
Dustin,

Just want to get ahead of this — one of our flavoring batches (FL-0198, Strawberry Natural) was flagged in a voluntary recall for elevated lead levels. 

This batch was NOT shipped to Gummy Co. Your last strawberry flavoring order was batch FL-0185, which is clean. I'm attaching the COA for FL-0185 so you have it on file.

No action needed on your end. Just wanted you to hear it from me first in case it hits industry news.

Steve
signals: compliance:flavoring recall FL-0198 does not affect us

subject: Q3 volume forecast — big numbers
body:
Dustin,

Wanted to give you early visibility on our Q3 forecast so you can plan production:

- Tropical Blast 10mg: 75,000 units/month (up from 50K)
- Blueberry Bliss 10mg: 40,000 units/month
- Watermelon Wave 5mg: 30,000 units/month
- NEW Sugar-Free line (if we proceed): 20,000 units/month per SKU

Total: ~225,000 units/month if sugar-free goes through, ~145,000 without.

Can your facility handle that volume? If we need to discuss capacity expansion or a second production run per week, let's get on a call next week.

Bob
